reasoning: With the forecast date at November 9, 2025, there are only about 50 days remaining before the January 1, 2026 resolution deadline. Generating a YES requires a public announcement by either the Chinese government or the US intelligence community regarding a Chinese military base in a BRICS country. Given the community prediction of 8% on October 30, it is highly likely that no such public announcement has been made yet. New military bases are typically massive projects that leave substantial footprints and generate years of speculation before any official public announcement. While there have been reports in the past regarding potential construction (such as alleged activities at Khalifa Port in the UAE, a recent BRICS member), the short time horizon remaining and the typically slow, deliberate pace of official intelligence or state announcements make it highly unlikely an announcement will occur in the final weeks of the year. The primary risk of a YES resolution would be an unexpected revelation in an annual US intelligence or Department of Defense report, but these normally drop earlier in the year or fall. Thus, the probability is very low.
